mission.title: envoy: Add X-Forwarded-Port header
mission.description: Add X-Forwarded-Port header. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: Add X-Forwarded-Port header

*Description*: Some applications rely on x-forwarded-* headers to regenerate the application URLs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add X-Forwarded-Port header
steps.description:
You can add the downstream port to the `X-Forwarded-Port` header by following config:
```
request_headers_to_add:
- header
    key: X-Forwarded-Port
    value: "%DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_PORT%"
```
```yaml
request_headers_to_add:
- header
    key: X-Forwarded-Port
    value: "%DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_PORT%"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add X-Forwarded-Port header" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
You can add the downstream port to the `X-Forwarded-Port` header by following config:
```
request_headers_to_add:
- header
    key: X-Forwarded-Port
    value: "%DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_PORT%"
```
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: EnvoyFilter
metadata:
  name: actuator-x-forward-port-header
  namespace: dev-team # as defined in meshConfig resource.
spec:
  configPatches:
    # The first patch adds the lua filter to the listener/http connection manager
  - applyTo: HTTP_FILTER
    match:
      context: SIDECAR_INBOUND
      listener:
        filterChain:
          filter:
            name: "envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager"
            subFilter:
              name: "envoy.filters.http.router"
    patch:
      operation: INSERT_BEFORE
      value: # lua filter specification
       name: envoy.filters.http.lua
       typed_config:
          "@type": "type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.lua.v3.Lua"
          inlineCode: |
            function env

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
